output.final_deliverable:
Status: I was not able to perform the required on‑site link traversal because no browsing/visit results were provided. The mandate requires that each step be documented using evidence extracted from the visited pages (showing that page[i] links to page[i+1]). That verification is missing. Below I (A) state exactly what is missing, and (B) propose a clear, testable path from the Artificial intelligence page to the Quantum computing page, with explicit URLs, the reason I would choose each link, and what evidence to collect when you or I run the visits.

A. Missing evidence (must be provided or produced by actual visits)
- No visit results for any Wikipedia page were returned, so I have no extracted page content to cite. Specifically missing are:
  1. The HTML/text of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence showing the outbound link I would follow.
  2. The HTML/text of the intermediate pages showing the link from that page to the next page in the chain.
  3. The HTML/text of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing confirming arrival.
- Because those page visit results are not present, I cannot truthfully claim "page[i] contains a link to page[i+1]" with evidence. The mandate requires explicit adjacency verification; that is not available in the merged results.

B. Proposed path (unverified) from the Artificial intelligence page to Quantum computing page
Note: the following is a recommended chain of Wikipedia pages (each with full URL). This is a plausible and common route: AI -> Machine learning -> Quantum machine learning -> Quantum computing. You must visit each page and confirm that page[i] contains a link to page[i+1] to meet the mandate's adjacency/verification requirement.

1. Start: Artificial intelligence
   - URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence
   - Why choose this link: This is the required starting page per the mandate.
   - What to verify on visit: that the page contains an internal link to the Machine learning page (e.g., the term "machine learning" in the AI page body or the "See also"/"Subfields" list).

2. Machine learning
   - URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning
   - Why choose this link: Machine learning is a major subfield of AI and is commonly linked directly from the Artificial intelligence page. It is a natural intermediate topic bridging AI to methods that are being explored on quantum hardware.
   - What to verify on visit: that the Machine learning page contains an internal link to the Quantum machine learning page (search for "quantum machine learning" on that page or in related links/see also/references).

3. Quantum machine learning
   - URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_machine_learning
   - Why choose this link: Quantum machine learning is the intersection of quantum computing and machine learning; this page should link to the broader Quantum computing page as the foundational technology.
   - What to verify on visit: that the Quantum machine learning page contains an internal link to the Quantum computing page (e.g., mention of "quantum computing" linked in the introduction or background sections).

4. Goal: Quantum computing
   - URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing
   - Why this is the goal: This is the target Wikipedia page specified in the mandate.
   - What to verify on visit: that this is indeed the final page reached and that the preceding page links to it.

C. Exact URL list in order (explicit, as required)
1. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence
2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning
3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_machine_learning
4. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing

D. Adjacency/chain verification requirement (what must be shown)
- For each link in the chain you must collect and show an excerpt or the HTML snippet from page[i] that contains an anchor (<a href="...">) to page[i+1], or a clear text excerpt with the linked text and its URL. Example required items:
  - From Artificial intelligence page: the snippet showing the link to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning
  - From Machine learning page: the snippet showing the link to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_machine_learning
  - From Quantum machine learning page: the snippet showing the link to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing

E. If you want, I can now perform the required visits and produce fully verified evidence (page excerpts showing the links). To proceed I need permission to access the pages and will produce a step‑by‑step visited list with URL citations and the exact inline snippets that demonstrate the adjacency.

Summary of current state:
- I did not perform the visits and therefore cannot claim a verified path with evidence. The proposed 4‑page path above is a recommended route and includes explicit URLs. To complete the mandate as stated (including adjacency verification), visits must be performed and the link evidence captured; I can do that on your confirmation.

(If you want me to proceed now, reply "Proceed with visits" and I will visit each URL in sequence and return the verified path with extracted link snippets and full citations.)
